releases.author: gharezlak
releases.description:
# Archivist Obsidian Importer v0.1.0

**Initial release** of the Archivist Obsidian Importer plugin. Import your Obsidian vault notes directly into your [Archivist](https://myarchivist.ai) campaigns with full markdown cleaning and wikilink preservation.

## Features

### 🎯 Smart Import System
- **Campaign Management**: List existing campaigns or create a new one matching your vault name
- **Selective Import**: Choose which files to import with checkboxes, shift-click ranges, and select-all
- **Flexible Type Mapping**: Designate each file as Player Character, NPC, Item, Location, Faction, or Lore
- **Lore Categories**: 50+ subtype options (World History, Magic System, House Rules, etc.) for organized lore

### ✨ Markdown Cleaning
- Automatically strips Obsidian-specific syntax before upload:
  - Dataview/DataviewJS queries
  - Callouts (`[!NOTE]`, `[!WARNING]`, etc.)
  - Inline fields (`key:: value`)
  - Comments (`%% ... %%`)
  - Images and embeds
  - YAML frontmatter
- Preserves standard markdown (headings, lists, emphasis, links, code)

### 🔗 Wikilink Preservation
- Extracts `[[wikilinks]]` from your notes
- Automatically creates relationship links in Archivist between imported entities
- Deduplicates and validates (only links entities in the import batch, skips Lore files)
- Respects aliases: `[[Target|Display Text]]`

### 📊 Progress Tracking
- Real-time progress bars for:
  - File imports (with individual file status)
  - Link creation (after import completes)
  - Campaign creation
- Clear visual feedback for all async operations

### 🧩 Smart Chunking
- Automatically splits large Lore files to respect API limits (2M char/request, 500K token world cap)
- Intelligent splitting by markdown headings/paragraphs
- Sequential naming for chunked files

## Installation

### Manual Installation (for testing)
1. Download `manifest.json`, `main.js`, and `styles.css` from this release
2. Create folder: `YourVault/.obsidian/plugins/archivist-importer/`
3. Copy the three files into that folder
4. Restart Obsidian
5. Go to Settings → Community plugins and enable "Archivist Importer"

### Configuration
1. Get your API key from [Archivist](https://app.myarchivist.ai/profile?section=dev)
2. Settings → Archivist Importer → paste your API key
3. Click the upload icon in the ribbon or use Command Palette: "Open Archivist Importer"

## Usage

1. **Select Campaign**: Choose an existing campaign or create a new one
2. **Select Files**: Check the files you want to import
3. **Set Types**: Choose the entity type for each file (Character, Item, Location, Faction, Lore)
4. **For Lore**: Select a subtype category from the dropdown
5. **Import**: Click "Import Selected" and watch the progress bars

Wikilinks between imported files will be automatically preserved as Archivist links!

## Requirements

- Obsidian 1.5.0 or higher
- Valid Archivist API key
- Desktop only (not tested on mobile)

## Privacy & Data

- API key stored locally in your vault
- No telemetry or analytics
- Network requests only to `https://api.myarchivist.ai` when you trigger actions
- Full details in [README.md](https://github.com/archivistai/archivist-obsidian-importer#privacy--data-usage)
- Archivist AI's [Privacy Policy](https://www.myarchivist.ai/privacy)

## Known Limitations

- Desktop only (mobile support not yet tested)
- Large vaults may take time to import (progress bars show status)
- Links only created for entities imported in the same batch

## Feedback & Issues

Report bugs or request features at: https://github.com/archivistai/archivist-obsidian-importer/issues

---

**Community Plugin Submission**: This plugin is being submitted to the Obsidian Community Plugins directory.

---
